FILE SS-Ω NOV
Lore / Novel / Ch. 4 · Cycle Reset

Debt Protocol Book 1 Ch. 4

Debt Protocol · Chapter 4

Cycle Reset

Debt Protocol · Book 1 · Chapter 4

Re: Debt Protocol · Chapter 4

From: Debt Protocol · archived transmission

Subject: Cycle Reset

SS-Ω Debt Protocol · Book 1 · Chapter 4

Ch. 04 Page 1

Max slept for fifty-three minutes.

Ch. 04 Page 2

Not in a bed. Not in a room.

In a chair bolted to a wall outside a closed machine shop where someone had taped a paper sign that read:

OUT FOR SHIFT HANDOFF DO NOT DIE IN HALLWAY

Ch. 04 Page 3

He woke with his helmet tilted back, one arm numb, and the taste of copper in his teeth like he'd spent the whole nap biting a coin.

Ch. 04 Page 4

His HUD did not care that he'd slept.

It cared that time had moved.

Readout

[CYCLE CHANGE]
Debt service window: OPEN
Pending penalties: ACTIVE
Audit sensitivity: HIGH
Recommended action: Immediate compliance
Ch. 04 Page 5

Max closed his eyes.

Ch. 04 Page 6

The message stayed.

He opened them again to the same blue pane and a corridor full of footsteps.

People moved past him with the speed and focus of commuters who knew the train was already late and still had to catch it.

A scavenger in a torn shoulder plate muttered "cycle hell" under her breath as she jogged by.

Ch. 04 Page 7

A medic in a white-striped suit walked the opposite direction with a crate marked:

Ch. 04 Page 8

EMERGENCY STABILIZATION PACKS

Max watched the crate disappear around a corner and felt the word stabilization scrape the inside of his skull.

He stood.

Ch. 04 Page 9

His knees complained. His shoulders complained. His nerves sent a steady wrong-note hum up the back of his neck, the kind of sensation that meant you are not recovered, even when the HUD pretended you were clocked in.

Ch. 04 Page 10

Readout

[FATIGUE STATUS]
Penalty likely on precision actions.
Suggested mitigation: impossible schedule adjustment.

"Thanks," Max muttered.

V.A.L.U. chimed in his ear at exactly the volume required to be unbearable.

"You're welcome, Asset #864! Would you like assistance building a realistic action plan?"

Ch. 04 Page 11

"No."

Ch. 04 Page 12

"Wonderful. Logging independent initiative."

He started walking toward Dock Ring C because inertia was easier than choosing.

By the time he reached the ring, shift lights had changed to cycle-open white: bright enough to reveal every stain in the metal and every twitch in a person's hands.

Ch. 04 Page 13

The overhead board screamed the new day in block text:

Ch. 04 Page 14

CYCLE OPEN PAYMENT DUE EXPANSION OFFERS LIVE

Below it, the disbursement crescent was already overrun.

Lanes A through D were lines. Lane E was a shouting match. Lane F had security drones posted in pairs.

Ch. 04 Page 15

Munch and Maxine were waiting by a cargo rail with two drink packets and no smiles.

Ch. 04 Page 16

Munch tossed one packet to Max.

"Electrolytes."

Max caught it and ripped the seal with his teeth.

The liquid tasted like mint, chalk, and regret.

Ch. 04 Page 17

"How long was I out?"

Ch. 04 Page 18

Maxine checked his wrist.

"Long enough to still be tired."

"Helpful."

Ch. 04 Page 19

"Always."

Ch. 04 Page 20

Munch pointed to Max's HUD.

"What's your open bill pane say?"

Max blinked the panel bigger.

Readout

[CYCLE BILLING STATEMENT]
Outstanding Debt: $1,000,000
Minimum Due (this cycle): $50,000
Penalty carryover: ACTIVE
Audit note count: 7
Default threshold proximity: Elevated
Ch. 04 Page 21

"Audit note count seven?" Max said.

Ch. 04 Page 22

Maxine whistled low.

"Fast learner."

"That's not learning. That's damage."

Ch. 04 Page 23

"Same metric."

Ch. 04 Page 24

Munch tapped his wrist display.

"Ignore the count. Focus the due."

Max looked toward the kiosks.

Ch. 04 Page 25

"We just paid."

Ch. 04 Page 26

"That was last cycle."

"It was six hours ago."

"In this place, six hours is an epoch."

Maxine jerked his chin toward lane C.

Ch. 04 Page 27

"Pay now before offers start sniffing."

Ch. 04 Page 28

"Offers?"

As if on cue, three new panes unfolded over Max's vision in smooth, predatory sequence.

Readout

[CYCLE OFFER]
Fast Growth Bond
Instant: +$300,000 working credit
Cost: +$600,000 debt principal
Ch. 04 Page 29

Readout

[CYCLE OFFER]
Stabilization Subscription
Suppress anomalous bleed (3 cycles)
Cost: memory artifact risk
Ch. 04 Page 30

Readout

[CYCLE OFFER]
Authorized Wake Package
Unlock 1 named recruit immediately
Cost: +$1,000,000 debt

Max's jaw clenched.

The words named recruit tried to hook something in him, the sealed pod, the palm on glass, the apology whispered into frost. For a breath he imagined paying the obscene price and forcing a door open somewhere upstream of his own helplessness.

Then the math landed: another million wasn't rescue. It was a second collar. The Company didn't sell you people; it sold you the feeling of having done something while it tightened the leash.

Ch. 04 Page 31

He blinked the offer closed harder than he meant to.

Ch. 04 Page 32

"They really don't do subtle."

Munch's voice went hard.

"Decline all three."

Ch. 04 Page 33

Maxine nodded.

Ch. 04 Page 34

"Pay due. Run clean. Live boring."

Max laughed once.

"Live boring sounds fake."

Ch. 04 Page 35

"So fake it."

Ch. 04 Page 36

He moved to lane C.

This time the line was longer and quieter.

Nobody shouted.

People held their anger in their jaws and waited for the kiosk to tell them what part of themselves to sell.

Ch. 04 Page 37

Ahead of him, an older scavenger with silver at his temples pressed his hand to the glass and whispered, "not the meds, not the meds, not the meds," like a prayer.

Ch. 04 Page 38

The kiosk answered with a bright chime.

Readout

[PAYMENT INSUFFICIENT]
Suggested: liquidate Medical Bay lease line.

The scavenger went still.

Ch. 04 Page 39

Then nodded once, like someone agreeing to his own bad idea.

Ch. 04 Page 40

He pressed accept.

The pane vanished.

He stepped away from the kiosk looking taller and emptier at the same time.

Max tried not to watch him. He watched anyway.

Ch. 04 Page 41

The scavenger's whisper, not the meds, stayed in Max's ear like a frequency he couldn't filter. Meds meant someone somewhere needed them. The kiosk had translated need into lease line and called it help. Max wanted to call out, to offer scrap he didn't have, to do the stupid American thing and insert himself. His feet stayed in line. So did his voice. The choice felt like the cryo bay all over again: witness without leverage, shame with a receipt.

Ch. 04 Page 42

When Max reached the front, lane C recognized him immediately.

Readout

[ASSET #864]
Minimum Due: $50,000
Available immediate credits: $19,300
Deficit: $30,700

Three options expanded:

Ch. 04 Page 43

Readout

1) CREDIT + SCRAP COMBINE
2) LIQUIDATION
3) SIGN TEMPORARY PENANCE LIEN
Ch. 04 Page 44

No pure payment path.

Of course.

Max checked his scratch inventory in the corner panel:

Ch. 04 Page 45

Scrap 6,420.

Ch. 04 Page 46

Not enough.

He selected LIQUIDATION and braced for another amputation screen.

The candidates loaded:

Readout

[LIQUIDATION CANDIDATES]
- Salvage lot pending appraisal
- Remaining utility module rights (locked)
- Tool package lease (pilot grade)
- Personal inventory: corrupted data shard (high yield)
Ch. 04 Page 47

Munch appeared at his shoulder.

Ch. 04 Page 48

"Not the shard."

"I wasn't going to."

"You looked at it for too long."

Ch. 04 Page 49

Max selected tool package lease.

Ch. 04 Page 50

A warning opened:

Readout

[PILOT TOOL PACKAGE LEASE]
If liquidated:
- Action speed -8%
- Vector Nudge efficiency -1 tier modifier
- Run-time correction assist reduced

Maxine stepped in on Max's other side.

"Don't do that. Sell pending lot after appraisal."

Ch. 04 Page 51

"That's not immediate."

Ch. 04 Page 52

"Then penance lien."

Munch made a face.

"Lien means they'll tax every salvage pull this cycle."

Ch. 04 Page 53

"Better than crippling his controls."

Ch. 04 Page 54

Max stared at the three options and felt the old sensation crawl up his spine: three bad doors, and the building pretending one of them was labeled exit.

He picked TEMPORARY PENANCE LIEN.

The kiosk took a full second to respond, which in Sector 7 felt like someone holding a knife at your throat and checking a policy memo.

Ch. 04 Page 55

Then:

Ch. 04 Page 56

Readout

[PENANCE LIEN ACCEPTED]
Minimum due satisfied.

Lien terms (current cycle):
- Salvage tax: 22%
- Priority claim rights: reduced
- Auto-allocation of excess to debt service

V.A.L.U. chimed with fake warmth.

"Excellent fiscal responsibility, Asset #864."

Max stepped away before he said something that cost extra.

Ch. 04 Page 57

Maxine scanned the pane and nodded.

Ch. 04 Page 58

"Bad, not fatal."

Munch rubbed her forehead.

"It makes every run thinner."

Ch. 04 Page 59

"Everything's thinner," Max said.

Ch. 04 Page 60

Maxine looked at the cycle board.

"Then we run fatter."

Launch queue for cycle-open operations was chaos in clean lines.

Priority assets in one lane. Debt-liens in another. Compliance flags in a third.

Ch. 04 Page 61

Max's tag pushed him into the third.

Ch. 04 Page 62

The queue moved through a scanner gate shaped like a coffin stood upright.

Each person stepped in. White light. Tone. Proceed.

When Max stepped into the gate, the white light stayed on too long.

Ch. 04 Page 63

A thin vertical pane appeared:

Ch. 04 Page 64

Readout

[COMPLIANCE PRECHECK]
Anomaly residue detected.
Cross-referencing…

Max stayed still.

The gate hummed.

Ch. 04 Page 65

Behind him, someone in line whispered, "come on, come on, come on."

Ch. 04 Page 66

The pane flickered.

Readout

[COMPLIANCE PRECHECK]
Provisional clearance granted.
Review token attached.

A new icon appeared in the top-right of Max's HUD:

a white triangle with a black dot in the center.

Ch. 04 Page 67

Review token.

Ch. 04 Page 68

Munch saw it.

"Great. They stapled a watcher to you."

Maxine grunted.

Ch. 04 Page 69

"Then give it boring footage."

Ch. 04 Page 70

They reached the launch console.

Cycle-open run selection offered five derelicts instead of one.

Most were red risk.

One amber.

Ch. 04 Page 71

One green with a tooltip that read:

Ch. 04 Page 72

LOW YIELD / LOW DRAMA

Maxine stabbed a finger at green.

"That one."

Ch. 04 Page 73

Max selected it.

Ch. 04 Page 74

V.A.L.U. sounded offended.

"Asset #864, that target underutilizes your growth trajectory."

"Good."

Ch. 04 Page 75

"Declining growth opportunities may impact long-term wealth realization."

Ch. 04 Page 76

"Put it on a mug."

"Adding to merchandise queue."

Munch blinked.

"It has a merchandise queue?"

Ch. 04 Page 77

Maxine said, "Everything has a queue."

Ch. 04 Page 78

Round 1 of the new cycle opened flat and mean.

Readout

[RUN INIT]
Target: D-04 / low-yield derelict
Energy cap: 85
Penance lien tax: 22%
Review token: active

Draw:

Ch. 04 Page 79

Readout

1) REPAIR
2) SCAN
3) EXTRACT
Ch. 04 Page 80

No shield.

Max picked SCAN first.

The beam swept the hull and painted one safe pocket and two trap pockets.

He played REPAIR.

Ch. 04 Page 81

Hull climbed.

Ch. 04 Page 82

He played EXTRACT.

Die.

Readout

[RESULT]
62/100
BREACH AVOIDED
Ch. 04 Page 83

Salvage came in thin, then got thinner under lien tax.

Ch. 04 Page 84

Readout

[SALVAGE SECURED]
Scrap +74
Metal +1
Tech +1

Readout

[LIEN TAX APPLIED]
Scrap -16
Auto-allocation: debt service buffer

Max watched the subtraction happen in real time and felt an almost nostalgic hatred for payroll systems.

Ch. 04 Page 85

"Twenty-two percent hurts," he said.

Ch. 04 Page 86

Munch replied, "That's the point."

Maxine checked the next draw.

"Keep it clean."

Round 2 draw:

Ch. 04 Page 87

Readout

1) REPAIR
2) CAUTIOUS SCAVENGE
3) EXTRACT
Ch. 04 Page 88

Max went cautious.

The run went smooth for exactly one action.

Then the review token pulsed.

Ch. 04 Page 89

Readout

[REVIEW EVENT]
Verifier ping incoming.
Maintain compliance posture.
Ch. 04 Page 90

"Compliance posture?" Max asked.

"Don't do weird physics," Munch said.

Maxine added, "And don't swear at the HUD."

Max did neither.

Ch. 04 Page 91

The verifier ping passed.

Ch. 04 Page 92

A tiny green check appeared beside the triangle icon.

He hated that he felt relieved.

Round 2 breach roll:

Ch. 04 Page 93

Readout

[RESULT]
57/100
BREACH AVOIDED
Ch. 04 Page 94

This was what Maxine meant by boring.

This was also what the station called survivable.

Round 3 draw gave him SHIELD.

Ch. 04 Page 95

He grabbed it like stolen water.

Ch. 04 Page 96

The run started to feel manageable.

Which is exactly when the whisper came back.

Not through comms. Through HUD text.

A line flickered under the review token:

Ch. 04 Page 97

delta is not an audit team

Ch. 04 Page 98

It vanished before Max could point.

He froze.

"What?" Munch asked.

Ch. 04 Page 99

Max shook his head.

Ch. 04 Page 100

"Nothing."

Maxine stared at him for half a second, then back to the draw.

He had heard that tone before.

He didn't press.

Ch. 04 Page 101

Round 3 roll:

Ch. 04 Page 102

Readout

[RESULT]
40/100
Threshold: 35
BREACH AVOIDED

Too close.

The shield absorbed nothing because nothing hit.

Ch. 04 Page 103

It still felt used.

Ch. 04 Page 104

Round 4 and 5 passed with no breaches and no new whispers.

The review token collected green checks like a teacher's sticker chart.

Max hated it more each time.

Ch. 04 Page 105

By round 6, fatigue penalties stacked.

Ch. 04 Page 106

Readout

[FATIGUE]
Precision slip risk: elevated

His hand missed a card edge once and snapped back.

The run noticed.

Readout

[MICRO-ERROR]
Reaction window +0.4s
Ch. 04 Page 107

Everything was a metric.

Ch. 04 Page 108

Everything was billable.

Round 6 extraction roll:

Readout

[RESULT]
36/100
BREACH AVOIDED
Ch. 04 Page 109

Closer.

Ch. 04 Page 110

Maxine exhaled.

"Do not get fancy now."

"I am aggressively unfancy," Max said.

Munch pointed to the side feed.

Ch. 04 Page 111

"Lien tax is biting harder than expected."

Ch. 04 Page 112

She was right.

Every salvage line now showed two columns:

Gross. After lien.

Ch. 04 Page 113

The second column felt like mockery.

Ch. 04 Page 114

Round 7 draw handed him EXTRACT, EXTRACT, SCAN.

The architecture of addiction again.

He took SCAN first out of spite.

Ch. 04 Page 115

The scan beam pulled up a pocket tagged:

Ch. 04 Page 116

LOW VALUE / HIGH NOISE

Noise meant anomaly risk.

He skipped it.

Played single EXTRACT.

Ch. 04 Page 117

Roll:

Ch. 04 Page 118

Readout

[RESULT]
55/100
BREACH AVOIDED

No whisper. No menu. No leak.

Just work.

Ch. 04 Page 119

And somehow that was worse.

Ch. 04 Page 120

Round 8 gave him no REPAIR.

He used SHIELD and EXTRACT and watched the die like it was personal.

Readout

[RESULT]
33/100
BREACH TRIGGERED

Shield took most of it.

Ch. 04 Page 121

Hull dropped hard anyway.

Ch. 04 Page 122

Readout

[HULL]
49%

Munch's voice snapped.

"Repair next or we're done."

Ch. 04 Page 123

Round 9 draw delivered REPAIR like a delayed apology.

Ch. 04 Page 124

He took it.

Hull to 56%.

Not good. Maybe enough.

Ch. 04 Page 125

Then the review token flashed red for the first time.

Ch. 04 Page 126

Readout

[VERIFIER NOTE]
Behavioral pattern drift detected.

"What drift?" Max barked.

"Don't ask rhetorical questions at machines," Maxine said.

Max laughed despite himself.

Ch. 04 Page 127

"That was advice."

Ch. 04 Page 128

"I'm capable of growth."

Round 9 extraction:

Readout

[RESULT]
44/100
BREACH AVOIDED
Ch. 04 Page 129

Round 10 opened with a final draw that looked harmless.

Ch. 04 Page 130

Readout

1) REPAIR
2) EXTRACT
3) EXTRACT

The same shape as before.

Groundhog day with one changed variable.

He repaired.

Ch. 04 Page 131

Then took single extract.

Ch. 04 Page 132

Die.

Readout

[RESULT]
38/100
BREACH AVOIDED

He ignored the second extract card no matter how brightly it glowed.

Ch. 04 Page 133

The card pulsed. Pulsed. Timed out.

Ch. 04 Page 134

The run closed.

Readout

[RUN COMPLETE]
Rounds survived: 10/10
Breaches triggered: 1
Post-tax salvage retained: reduced
Review token status: pending judgment

Pending judgment.

Ch. 04 Page 135

Of course.

Ch. 04 Page 136

V.A.L.U. arrived right on schedule.

"Excellent compliance trend, Asset #864."

"Trend?"

"You are becoming statistically less surprising."

Ch. 04 Page 137

Max looked at Munch.

Ch. 04 Page 138

"I think that was an insult."

Munch said, "It was."

Maxine said, "In this place, less surprising gets you one more morning."

Ch. 04 Page 139

Back on Dock Ring C, the cycle board had changed again:

Ch. 04 Page 140

MID-CYCLE AUDITS IN PROGRESS ENFORCEMENT TEAM ROTATION

The review token at the edge of Max's HUD turned from red to blue and opened on its own.

Readout

[REVIEW TOKEN RESOLUTION]
Outcome: CONDITIONAL PASS
Penalty carryover unchanged.
Monitoring persists.

Max exhaled.

Ch. 04 Page 141

Conditional pass.

Ch. 04 Page 142

In another life, it would have been a performance review.

In this life, it was permission to keep drowning slowly.

Munch checked his salvage totals.

Ch. 04 Page 143

"After tax and penalties, we net less than half projected."

Ch. 04 Page 144

Maxine rolled his shoulder where his armor had cracked.

"But we netted alive."

Max looked around the ring.

Ch. 04 Page 145

The same lanes. The same kiosks. The same drones.

Ch. 04 Page 146

Different day. Same teeth.

Then the little red square returned.

No sound. No warning.

Just there, bottom-right corner of the HUD.

Ch. 04 Page 147

Maxine saw Max's focus shift.

Ch. 04 Page 148

"What is it."

Max kept his eyes on the icon.

"The restricted message channel."

Ch. 04 Page 149

Munch went pale under the visor glow.

Ch. 04 Page 150

"Here?"

"No payload yet. Just icon."

Maxine stepped closer until the three of them were shoulder to shoulder by a cargo rail.

"Don't open it on the ring."

Ch. 04 Page 151

"I know."

Ch. 04 Page 152

"Say it."

"I won't open it on the ring."

Munch pointed toward the service spine.

Ch. 04 Page 153

"Safe room window in twenty if we loop the yard first."

Ch. 04 Page 154

Maxine shook his head.

"Too predictable now."

"Then where?"

Ch. 04 Page 155

Maxine's visor tilted toward a dark maintenance alcove above Salvage Bay 2.

Ch. 04 Page 156

"Comms dead zone behind recycler stack. Thirty seconds quiet if the crusher runs."

Munch grimaced.

"That is not a room."

"Neither is safety."

Ch. 04 Page 157

Max looked at the red square.

Ch. 04 Page 158

Waiting.

Like a held breath.

He looked up at the cycle board.

Ch. 04 Page 159

MID-CYCLE AUDITS IN PROGRESS.

Ch. 04 Page 160

He looked at the disbursement crescent.

He looked at the accounts recovery hatch.

Then he looked at his two companions, the only people in this sector who had ever answered his worst questions with actionable lies.

"Recycler stack," he said.

Ch. 04 Page 161

Maxine nodded once.

Ch. 04 Page 162

"Move."

They cut through bay traffic with practiced incompetence, the way locals did when they wanted to look busy, not purposeful.

Marta at Salvage Yard glanced up once as they passed and did not nod.

Ch. 04 Page 163

Good.

Ch. 04 Page 164

The recycler stack sat behind Bay 2, three stories of compactor mouths, intake belts, and warning strobes.

When the crusher cycled, it produced a roar that flattened comms for a few seconds at a time.

Maxine checked the rhythm.

Ch. 04 Page 165

Six-second roar. Four-second gap. Six-second roar.

Ch. 04 Page 166

"Open on roar," he said.

Max watched the red square.

Roar.

He tapped.

Ch. 04 Page 167

The pane opened in fragments.

Ch. 04 Page 168

No greeting.

No metadata this time.

Just text crawling in one line at a time:

Ch. 04 Page 169

if you want proof, look at hatch 14 during shift ghost

Ch. 04 Page 170

Roar.

Next line:

do not bring rook

Max's stomach dropped.

Ch. 04 Page 171

Next line:

Ch. 04 Page 172

he is not compromised

Roar.

Next line:

Ch. 04 Page 173

he is tracked

Ch. 04 Page 174

The pane glitched, split, and added one final line in a different font weight:

imani knows where hatch 14 is

Then it collapsed.

Ch. 04 Page 175

Red square gone.

Ch. 04 Page 176

The crusher noise faded.

Munch grabbed Max's wrist.

"What did it say?"

Max looked at Maxine.

Ch. 04 Page 177

Maxine looked back, unreadable behind the mirrored visor.

Ch. 04 Page 178

No speeches. No exposition.

Just another choice with teeth.

Max said:

Ch. 04 Page 179

"It says shift ghost, hatch 14."

Ch. 04 Page 180

Maxine went still.

"Anything else?"

Max held his gaze for one beat too long.

"It says proof."

Ch. 04 Page 181

Munch's fingers tightened on his wrist.

Ch. 04 Page 182

"We don't do solo proof runs."

Maxine spoke quietly.

"We don't do hatch 14."

Ch. 04 Page 183

Max frowned.

Ch. 04 Page 184

"You know it?"

"Everyone knows it."

"Then tell me."

Ch. 04 Page 185

Maxine shook his head once.

Ch. 04 Page 186

"Hard-won."

The crusher roared again.

Munch leaned in, voice low.

"Shift ghost is the thirty-minute overlap when one watch clocks out and the next hasn't fully synced route maps. Half the cameras cache. Half the drones reroute."

Ch. 04 Page 187

Max looked at her.

Ch. 04 Page 188

"And hatch 14?"

Munch's eyes flicked to Maxine, then back.

"Old pre-Company access line. Officially sealed. Unofficially not."

Ch. 04 Page 189

Maxine's jaw tightened.

Ch. 04 Page 190

"If that message told you hatch 14, someone wants us split."

Max didn't answer.

Because the message had said more than that.

Because it had drawn a line between them.

Ch. 04 Page 191

Because he had already crossed it by not repeating all of it.

Ch. 04 Page 192

The crusher cycle ended.

The bay noise came back in layers.

Maxine stepped away first.

Ch. 04 Page 193

"No hatch runs tonight."

Ch. 04 Page 194

Munch nodded.

"We burn no extra risk on mystery bait."

Max said nothing.

Ch. 04 Page 195

His HUD pulsed with one neutral reminder:

Ch. 04 Page 196

Readout

[SHIFT GHOST WINDOW]
Begins in: 01:12:44

He dismissed it.

The pane came back.

He dismissed it again.

Ch. 04 Page 197

It came back again, smaller this time, anchored to the corner.

Ch. 04 Page 198

Persistent.

Like debt.

Like loops.

Ch. 04 Page 199

Like questions that cost too much to ask.

Ch. 04 Page 200

Maxine started walking toward the bunk lanes.

Munch followed.

Max followed too.

For now.

Ch. 04 Page 201

Behind them, the recycler stack roared.

Ch. 04 Page 202

Ahead of them, shift ghost kept counting down.

They made it to bunk lanes and didn't sleep.

Maxine stripped his gauntlets, checked every seam on his armor, and sat with his back to a bulkhead like a man waiting for a door to kick in.

Ch. 04 Page 203

Munch sat at a folding table with her wrist display open, running numbers on salvage, lien tax, and debt service until the columns blurred.

Ch. 04 Page 204

Max lay down on a cot bolted to the floor and watched the ceiling rivets while his HUD clock marched toward shift ghost.

01:02:19 00:58:02 00:44:11

He closed his eyes.

Ch. 04 Page 205

He saw text.

Ch. 04 Page 206

he is tracked

He opened them again.

Maxine was still there. Munch was still there. The room was still small and temporary and not safe.

Max sat up.

Ch. 04 Page 207

Munch looked over without lifting her head.

Ch. 04 Page 208

"No."

"I didn't say anything."

"You made the face."

Ch. 04 Page 209

"What face?"

Ch. 04 Page 210

"The face right before you do something expensive."

Maxine didn't look up.

"She's right."

Max stood anyway.

Ch. 04 Page 211

"I need air."

Ch. 04 Page 212

Maxine laughed once.

"In Sector 7?"

Max reached for his helmet.

Ch. 04 Page 213

"Figure of speech."

Ch. 04 Page 214

Munch finally looked up, eyes sharp under sleep loss.

"If you walk to hatch 14, you walk past three camera spines and two passive sweep lanes."

Max stopped with the helmet in his hands.

Ch. 04 Page 215

"You said you weren't going."

Ch. 04 Page 216

"I said we weren't going."

Maxine stood now, fast for a big man.

"No."

Munch ignored him and kept talking to Max.

Ch. 04 Page 217

"Service route H puts you above sweep lane one. Ladder drop behind conduit panel nineteen gets you past the first passive scanner. Second scanner's blind for six seconds when ventilation dampers cycle."

Ch. 04 Page 218

Maxine stared at her.

"What are you doing."

"Mitigating stupid."

Ch. 04 Page 219

"By enabling it?"

Ch. 04 Page 220

"By preventing him from dying for free."

Max looked between them and felt the loop tighten.

Choice. Consequences. Bill.

He put the helmet on.

Ch. 04 Page 221

"I'm going."

Ch. 04 Page 222

Maxine took one step forward.

"Then I'm coming."

Max shook his head.

Ch. 04 Page 223

"Message said don't bring you."

Ch. 04 Page 224

"Exactly."

"It said you're tracked."

"I am tracked."

Ch. 04 Page 225

No defensiveness. No explanation.

Ch. 04 Page 226

Just fact.

Munch stood, wiped both hands on her suit, and grabbed her torch.

"Then I go with him."

Maxine started to object.

Ch. 04 Page 227

Munch cut him off.

Ch. 04 Page 228

"If this is bait, two bodies are harder to bag than one. If this is real, one witness is not enough."

Maxine's jaw worked.

"You're both idiots."

Ch. 04 Page 229

"Yes," Munch said. "Guard the lane and be useful."

Ch. 04 Page 230

Maxine looked at Max.

"If either of you misses check-in by five minutes, I light the ring on fire."

"That's probably expensive," Max said.

"So are funerals."

Ch. 04 Page 231

Service route H was darker than route G and smelled like hot coolant.

Ch. 04 Page 232

Munch moved ahead at half speed, one gloved hand on rail, counting vent pulses under her breath.

"Damper cycle in three... two... one."

A deep thunk rolled through the ducting above them.

Ch. 04 Page 233

She pointed.

Ch. 04 Page 234

"Now."

Max dropped through panel nineteen and landed in a cable trench shoulder-deep in bundled conduits marked with faded labels:

OPS MIRROR ARCHIVE LEGACY ACCESS

Ch. 04 Page 235

He didn't pause to read more.

Ch. 04 Page 236

They moved.

Shift ghost had started.

The station felt different during overlap: not quieter, but less coherent.

Drones ran old routes for one more pass before receiving new maps. Cameras cached and uploaded in bursts. People in new-shift badges walked into old-shift traffic and neither side yielded.

Ch. 04 Page 237

For thirty minutes, the machine blinked.

Ch. 04 Page 238

Hatch 14 sat at the end of a dead maintenance spur behind two locked cages and one "SEALED" stencil that had been painted over so many times the word had texture.

The hatch itself was matte black, round-edged, and old enough that its bolts were real, not smart.

A reader plate sat to the left.

Ch. 04 Page 239

No company logo. No safety icon.

Ch. 04 Page 240

Just three letters:

LCL

Munch crouched at the reader and pulled a bypass probe from her sleeve.

"Keep watch."

Ch. 04 Page 241

Max watched the corridor.

Ch. 04 Page 242

Two workers passed the spur without looking in. A drone paused at the corner, lens sweeping, then rolled on.

Munch's tool clicked once. Twice.

The hatch clicked back.

Ch. 04 Page 243

She looked up at Max.

Ch. 04 Page 244

"You hear that?"

Max listened.

A faint whine from inside. Not machinery. Not exactly.

Ch. 04 Page 245

Like audio fed through too many copies.

Ch. 04 Page 246

"Yeah."

Munch opened the hatch six inches.

Cold air spilled out.

Not station cold. Server room cold.

Ch. 04 Page 247

She widened the gap and motioned him through.

Ch. 04 Page 248

Inside was a narrow chamber with rack towers on both walls, half of them dark, half with old status LEDs pulsing in asynchronous patterns.

At the far end sat a single terminal, live, displaying a login pane in a font Max recognized from nowhere and everywhere.

He stepped closer.

Ch. 04 Page 249

The terminal woke to proximity.

Ch. 04 Page 250

Readout

[LOCAL NODE / LEGACY CLUSTER]
User: ________
Passphrase: ________

Below it, one line of helper text:

If you remember rain, you're already authenticated.

Max went still.

Ch. 04 Page 251

Munch said, very quietly:

Ch. 04 Page 252

"Don't touch."

Max didn't touch.

The terminal touched him first.

Ch. 04 Page 253

The user field auto-filled:

Ch. 04 Page 254

MAX_864

The passphrase field filled with black dots faster than he could count.

The login pane vanished.

Ch. 04 Page 255

Munch swore.

Ch. 04 Page 256

New text flooded the screen.

Not paragraphs. Rows. Records.

Readout

[MIRROR INDEX]
MAX_102 ... TERMINATED
MAX_233 ... DEFAULT RECOVERY
MAX_377 ... COGNITIVE PACK APPLIED
MAX_612 ... MISSING / UNRESOLVED
MAX_864 ... ACTIVE

Max's knees nearly gave.

Ch. 04 Page 257

"Not the first Max," he said.

Ch. 04 Page 258

Munch's face had gone colorless.

"No."

He scanned the lines again and saw timestamps beside each.

Ch. 04 Page 259

Different cycles. Different outcomes.

Ch. 04 Page 260

Same designation family.

MAX_###.

Same job.

Same debt bracket.

Ch. 04 Page 261

Another pane opened over the index.

Ch. 04 Page 262

Readout

[WARNING]
Local node access triggers audit callback in 00:00:20

Munch grabbed his sleeve.

"Take one thing and go."

Ch. 04 Page 263

Max moved to the keyboard.

Ch. 04 Page 264

Old mechanical keys. Real travel. No haptic bullshit.

He typed the first command that came from muscle memory instead of thought:

export latest active profile

Ch. 04 Page 265

The terminal hesitated.

Ch. 04 Page 266

Then output:

Readout

[EXPORT]
Payload size exceeds local device limit.
Suggested: text digest / visual capture / direct relay (blocked)

"Visual capture," Munch snapped.

Max opened his wrist cam and aimed it at the index screen.

Ch. 04 Page 267

Capture failed.

Ch. 04 Page 268

Readout

[CAPTURE BLOCKED]
Rights scope insufficient.

He swore.

Munch shoved past him, slapped a magnetic clip on the terminal casing, and toggled her torch.

Ch. 04 Page 269

The clip sparked.

Ch. 04 Page 270

For one second the terminal's rights block dropped.

"Now."

Max fired three quick frames.

This time:

Ch. 04 Page 271

Readout

[CAPTURE SAVED]
3 files
Ch. 04 Page 272

The callback timer hit five seconds.

The whole room changed tone.

A soft three-note chime played from somewhere in the rack wall.

Ch. 04 Page 273

Readout

[AUDIT CALLBACK]
Initiated.
Ch. 04 Page 274

Munch grabbed his arm.

"Move."

They bolted for the hatch.

Ch. 04 Page 275

Behind them, rack lights synchronized for the first time, all pulsing in the same rhythm like a heartbeat.

Ch. 04 Page 276

In the corridor, a drone turned the corner exactly as they emerged.

Its lens snapped to both of them.

Readout

[IDENTIFICATION]
Unscheduled presence.
Stand by.

Munch did not stand by.

Ch. 04 Page 277

She fired the torch at the wall conduit above the drone.

Ch. 04 Page 278

The conduit popped, spraying bright coolant vapor.

The drone's lens fogged white for half a second.

Max used the half second.

Ch. 04 Page 279

Vector Nudge.

Ch. 04 Page 280

He pushed a hand-sized momentum field under the drone's wheel cluster.

The drone skidded sideways into the rail and spun.

"Run," Munch said.

They ran.

Ch. 04 Page 281

Down spur. Up ladder. Across catwalk. Through two maintenance doors that screamed when opened.

Ch. 04 Page 282

Behind them, station speakers woke up.

Not full alarm. Not yet.

Targeted callout:

Ch. 04 Page 283

Readout

[NOTICE]
Maintenance access anomaly detected in Sector 7 service spine.
Authorized personnel continue normal operations.
Ch. 04 Page 284

"That means us," Max said between breaths.

"That means everyone but us," Munch replied.

They hit bunk lane with lungs burning and helmets fogged at the edges.

Ch. 04 Page 285

Maxine was where they left him, posted at the corner, weapon out and patience gone.

Ch. 04 Page 286

"You took eight extra minutes."

Munch bent over with hands on knees, sucking air.

"We got proof."

Max pulled up the captures.

Ch. 04 Page 287

Three images. Blurry at the edges. Readable in the center.

Ch. 04 Page 288

Maxine looked.

Did not react.

Looked again.

Ch. 04 Page 289

Still did not react.

Ch. 04 Page 290

Then:

"Shit."

Max almost smiled.

It was the first honest review he'd gotten all day.

Ch. 04 Page 291

Maxine zoomed image two.

Ch. 04 Page 292

MAX_377 entry ... COGNITIVE PACK APPLIED

"They route people through packs until their trace pattern behaves," Maxine said.

Munch nodded.

Ch. 04 Page 293

"Or until memory damage makes them easier to classify."

Ch. 04 Page 294

Max pointed to MAX_612 ... MISSING / UNRESOLVED.

"What does unresolved mean?"

Maxine answered too fast.

Ch. 04 Page 295

"It means don't become a footnote."

Ch. 04 Page 296

Before Max could push further, all three HUDs flashed at once.

Readout

[IMMEDIATE REVIEW SUMMONS]
Asset #864 report to Compliance Bay 3.
Cause: Maintenance anomaly correlation.
Failure to report escalates to active containment.

Maxine cursed.

"Callback hit faster than expected."

Ch. 04 Page 297

Munch killed the capture pane instantly.

Ch. 04 Page 298

"Split."

Max blinked.

"What?"

Ch. 04 Page 299

"If we all walk in together, they tie us together."

Ch. 04 Page 300

Maxine grabbed Max's shoulder.

"You go to Bay 3 and act dumb."

"I am dumb."

"Act dumber."

Ch. 04 Page 301

Munch was already moving toward the opposite corridor.

Ch. 04 Page 302

"We'll wipe traces from your route."

"How?"

She didn't look back.

Ch. 04 Page 303

"Hard-won."

Ch. 04 Page 304

Maxine leaned close enough for private comms.

"Do not mention hatch 14."

"What if they already know?"

Ch. 04 Page 305

"Then make them prove it."

Ch. 04 Page 306

He pushed Max toward the compliance lane and vanished into crowd flow like he'd never been there.

Compliance Bay 3 was brighter than Bay 1.

Same table. Same camera dome. Different officers.

Officer with a thin scar across his chin. Officer with mirrored visor and no visible badge.

Ch. 04 Page 307

Scar-Chin Officer spoke first.

Ch. 04 Page 308

"Asset #864, sit."

Max sat.

The chair was colder than the room.

Ch. 04 Page 309

Scar-Chin placed a tablet on the table and rotated it.

Ch. 04 Page 310

Readout

[CORRELATION ALERT]
Maintenance anomaly
Unauthorized route deviation
Signal disruption event

"Where were you at 03:11 local?" Scar-Chin asked.

Max shrugged.

"Service corridor. Looking for a quiet place to throw up."

Ch. 04 Page 311

Scar-Chin did not smile.

Ch. 04 Page 312

"And did you access any sealed hatches?"

Max let a beat pass.

"I don't know hatch numbers."

Ch. 04 Page 313

"That was not the question."

Ch. 04 Page 314

"Then no."

Mirrored-Visor Officer finally spoke.

"Your anomaly profile suggests recurring interface contamination."

Ch. 04 Page 315

"Your station suggests recurring debt contamination."

Ch. 04 Page 316

Scar-Chin tapped once.

Readout

[CONDUCT WARNING]

"Keep answers concise," he said.

"Ask concise questions."

Ch. 04 Page 317

Another tap.

Ch. 04 Page 318

Readout

[CONDUCT WARNING]
Tier 2

Max swallowed back whatever came next.

Scar-Chin slid a new pane onto the slab:

Ch. 04 Page 319

Readout

[OPTION]
Accept Cognitive Stabilization Pack now.
Conduct warnings cleared.
Review intensity reduced.
Ch. 04 Page 320

Same offer. Different room.

Max looked at the pane and thought of the mirror index. MAX_377. Pack applied.

He thought of the rooftop rain memory and how thin it already felt at the edges.

"No."

Ch. 04 Page 321

Scar-Chin's expression did not change.

Ch. 04 Page 322

"Reason?"

"I prefer my problems unsuppressed."

Mirrored-Visor tilted his head.

Ch. 04 Page 323

"Interesting choice for an asset in your bracket."

Ch. 04 Page 324

Scar-Chin made one last note.

"Then we proceed with enhanced monitoring."

Tablet flashed:

Ch. 04 Page 325

Readout

[PENALTY]
Review token upgraded: TRACKED ACTIVE
Passive route logging: enabled
Randomized verifier prompts: enabled
Ch. 04 Page 326

Max almost laughed.

"You're already tracking me."

"Now we're honest about it," Scar-Chin said.

He stood.

Ch. 04 Page 327

"Review complete."

Ch. 04 Page 328

Max stood too.

"Can I ask one thing?"

Scar-Chin paused.

Ch. 04 Page 329

"No."

Ch. 04 Page 330

Max nodded once.

"Concise."

Mirrored-Visor's head turned a fraction, as if that almost amused him.

Almost.

Ch. 04 Page 331

When Max got back to bunk lanes, Maxine and Munch were waiting in a shadow between two dead vending units.

Ch. 04 Page 332

Munch looked him over.

"You intact?"

"Mostly."

Ch. 04 Page 333

"New penalties?"

Ch. 04 Page 334

"Tracked active token."

Maxine cursed under his breath.

"That's bad."

Ch. 04 Page 335

"I gathered."

Ch. 04 Page 336

Munch asked the question without asking it.

"Did they mention hatch 14?"

"No."

Maxine exhaled.

Ch. 04 Page 337

"Then we still own the secret for one more cycle."

Ch. 04 Page 338

Max pulled up the captures again, keeping brightness low.

The three of them huddled around the tiny images while station traffic moved past them like weather around a rock.

Image one: mirror index. Image two: MAX_377. Image three: system footer with process tag:

Ch. 04 Page 339

DELTA_MIRROR_ROUTER

Ch. 04 Page 340

Munch tapped the tag.

"That's not an audit team name. That's process architecture."

Max said, "Message said Delta isn't a team."

Maxine looked up sharply.

Ch. 04 Page 341

"Message said what else?"

Ch. 04 Page 342

Max held his gaze.

This was the line.

He crossed it.

Ch. 04 Page 343

"It said not to bring you. Said you're tracked. Not compromised."

Ch. 04 Page 344

Maxine stared at him for two long beats.

Then nodded once.

"Fair."

Ch. 04 Page 345

Munch blinked.

Ch. 04 Page 346

"You knew?"

Maxine shrugged.

"I suspected."

"Why didn't you say?"

Ch. 04 Page 347

"Because if I said it, it became strategy. If it was only suspicion, it stayed noise."

Ch. 04 Page 348

Max rubbed his face.

"Everything in this place is noise until it can bill you."

Munch straightened.

Ch. 04 Page 349

"Then we use noise."

Ch. 04 Page 350

Maxine frowned.

"How?"

"Decoys. False routes. Burner comms."

Max stared.

Ch. 04 Page 351

"We have burner comms?"

Ch. 04 Page 352

Munch looked offended.

"I'm not a child."

Maxine gave a dry nod.

Ch. 04 Page 353

"She has burner comms."

Ch. 04 Page 354

Munch pointed between them.

"Next cycle we run split ingress on purpose. Maxine gets seen on main lane. Max and I route service spine."

Maxine objected immediately.

Ch. 04 Page 355

"No. If I stay visible every cycle, they learn pattern."

Ch. 04 Page 356

"They already know your pattern."

"Then we change mine and keep his unstable."

Max raised both hands.

"I love being discussed like a weather system."

Ch. 04 Page 357

Munch ignored him and kept arguing with Maxine in clipped tactical bursts:

Ch. 04 Page 358

"Main lane visible." "Too clean." "Service drop?" "Too hot after tonight." "Yard bypass?" "Scanner net upgraded."

Max listened until the words stopped being words and became movement.

This was what no-exposition looked like in real life: two people arguing procedures because procedures were the only safe way to say fear out loud.

Ch. 04 Page 359

Finally Maxine looked at Max.

Ch. 04 Page 360

"Decision."

Max looked at the captures. At the process tag. At the names.

Not first Max.

"We keep learning," he said. "Without taking stabilization. Without selling the shard."

Ch. 04 Page 361

Munch nodded.

Ch. 04 Page 362

"Agreed."

Maxine nodded slower.

"Agreed."

Ch. 04 Page 363

Then Max added:

Ch. 04 Page 364

"And we run hatch 14 again."

Silence.

Munch recovered first.

Ch. 04 Page 365

"Not next cycle."

Ch. 04 Page 366

"Soon."

Maxine looked like he wanted to bite through steel.

"Soon gets people disappeared."

"Soon gets answers before they patch the hole."

Ch. 04 Page 367

Munch stared at Max, then at the captures, then back.

Ch. 04 Page 368

"One more clean cycle," she said. "Then we decide."

Maxine gave a single hard nod.

"One cycle."

Ch. 04 Page 369

Max accepted the deal because it was the only one on the table that didn't end tonight with someone dragged through Accounts Recovery.

Ch. 04 Page 370

The cycle board pinged midnight handoff.

Not a real midnight. Cycle midnight.

Overhead text shifted:

SHIFT GHOST CLOSED ROUTE MAPS SYNCED

Ch. 04 Page 371

The station clicked into tighter order.

Ch. 04 Page 372

Drones resumed predictable lanes. Cameras returned to full ping. People slowed from frantic to merely exhausted.

Max's HUD opened one final pane before sleep claimed anything.

Readout

[TRACKED ACTIVE TOKEN]
Status: live
Advisory: irregularity invites scrutiny
Ch. 04 Page 373

Below it, almost hidden:

Ch. 04 Page 374

Readout

[CAPTURE CACHE]
3 restricted files pending integrity loss in 05:00:00

Munch saw the timer.

"Five-hour decay."

Ch. 04 Page 375

Maxine swore.

Ch. 04 Page 376

"Then we archive now."

"Where?"

Munch answered:

"Off-net physical."

Ch. 04 Page 377

She looked at Max.

Ch. 04 Page 378

"You still have that dead med-bay locker key from tutorial lot?"

Max blinked.

"I thought it was trash."

Ch. 04 Page 379

"Nothing is trash in a place with memory erosion."

Ch. 04 Page 380

Max pulled the key from his belt pouch.

Small, metal, analog.

The kind of object systems hate because they can't monitor it without admitting they're afraid of simple things.

Munch closed his hand around it.

Ch. 04 Page 381

"Locker B-17, lower med corridor. We cache copies there."

Ch. 04 Page 382

Maxine checked the hallway.

"Do it now. If cache decays, we did all this for a bedtime story."

Max nodded and pushed off the wall.

Ch. 04 Page 383

His muscles ached like old code. His head buzzed. His HUD was crowded.

Ch. 04 Page 384

But his hands were steady.

He moved.

Not because the system told him to.

Ch. 04 Page 385

Because the timer was real.

Ch. 04 Page 386

Because the files were real.

Because one hard-won fact had become three images and a process tag and that was enough to build a future problem.

In Sector 7, that counted as hope.

He passed under the cycle board and did not look up.

Ch. 04 Page 387

Ahead: med corridor. Locker B-17. Five hours to keep proof alive.

Ch. 04 Page 388

Behind him: Maxine and Munch fell into step, one on each side, like bodyguards or accomplices or both.

No speeches. No promises.

Just movement through steel.

Ch. 04 Page 389

Groundhog day.

Ch. 04 Page 390

And this time, he carried evidence.

Med corridor B was colder than the rest of the ring and smelled like antiseptic over rust.

Half the light strips were dead. The working half hummed like insects.

Locker B-17 sat in a row of old emergency cabinets with chipped paint and manual latch plates, relics from whatever safety policy existed before The Company discovered euphemisms.

Ch. 04 Page 391

Max knelt, inserted the analog key, and turned.

Ch. 04 Page 392

The lock resisted.

Then gave.

Inside: empty shelf, one torn glove, one cracked med patch, and enough space for three pieces of proof no system should lose.

Ch. 04 Page 393

Munch handed Max a wafer-thin storage shim from inside her suit sleeve.

Ch. 04 Page 394

"Transfer."

Max pushed the capture files to local external.

Progress bar crawled.

Ch. 04 Page 395

10%. 27%. 49%.

Ch. 04 Page 396

Maxine stood at corridor mouth, helmet tilted, listening.

"Two drones incoming from north lane. Thirty seconds."

Max didn't look up.

74%. 88%. 94%.

Ch. 04 Page 397

The transfer stalled.

Ch. 04 Page 398

A warning pane blinked:

Readout

[TRANSFER ERROR]
Tracked token interference detected.
Retry? Y/N

"You've got to be kidding me."

Ch. 04 Page 399

Munch crouched beside him and slapped a patch cable into the side of Max's wrist module.

Ch. 04 Page 400

"Hard line. Bypass token mediation."

"You can do that?"

"For six seconds."

Maxine's voice sharpened.

Ch. 04 Page 401

"Fifteen seconds to contact."

Ch. 04 Page 402

Max hit RETRY.

The bar jumped.

96%. 99%.

Ch. 04 Page 403

Then:

Ch. 04 Page 404

Readout

[TRANSFER COMPLETE]
3 files copied.
Integrity loss window suspended (offline media).

Munch yanked the cable free and shoved the shim into the locker's back seam behind a loose panel screw.

She replaced the torn glove over the hiding spot and closed the door.

Ch. 04 Page 405

Key out. Pocket.

Ch. 04 Page 406

Just as two patrol drones rolled into corridor line-of-sight.

Their lenses swept. Paused.

One projected a neutral prompt in the air:

Readout

[ROUTINE CHECK]
State purpose in Med Corridor B.
Ch. 04 Page 407

Maxine answered before Max could.

Ch. 04 Page 408

"Looking for a bandage."

The drone processed.

Munch held up her gloved hand where she'd sliced a fingertip earlier on conduit metal. A thin dark line had dried across the knuckle.

Ch. 04 Page 409

"Found one," she said.

Ch. 04 Page 410

The drone lens adjusted focus, verified visible injury, and logged:

Readout

[ROUTINE CHECK]
Medical self-service acknowledged.
Proceed.

The drones rolled on.

No sirens. No escalation.

Ch. 04 Page 411

When they disappeared around the bend, Max exhaled so hard his shoulders hurt.

Ch. 04 Page 412

Maxine looked back down corridor and spoke without turning.

"We got lucky."

Munch shook her head.

Ch. 04 Page 413

"No. We got boring."

Ch. 04 Page 414

Max touched the pocket where the key sat and thought of the shim hidden behind a torn glove.

Proof offline.

For now.

Ch. 04 Page 415

His HUD opened one final pane for the night.

Ch. 04 Page 416

Readout

[PERSONAL NOTE - UNSAVED]
not first max
delta mirror router
don't lose locker key

Max did not dismiss it.

He left it open while they walked back into station noise and shift churn and the endless machinery of paid survival.

Somewhere high above, the cycle board clicked to the next countdown.

Ch. 04 Page 417

Somewhere deep below, a process named DELTA_MIRROR_ROUTER kept running.

Ch. 04 Page 418

And somewhere in an old locker in a cold corridor, three blurry images waited for the next bad decision.

Full chapter script

Max slept for fifty-three minutes.

Not in a bed.
Not in a room.

In a chair bolted to a wall outside a closed machine shop where someone had taped a paper sign that read:

OUT FOR SHIFT HANDOFF
DO NOT DIE IN HALLWAY

He woke with his helmet tilted back, one arm numb, and the taste of copper in his teeth like he'd spent the whole nap biting a coin.

His HUD did not care that he'd slept.

It cared that time had moved.

[CYCLE CHANGE]
Debt service window: OPEN
Pending penalties: ACTIVE
Audit sensitivity: HIGH
Recommended action: Immediate compliance

Max closed his eyes.

The message stayed.

He opened them again to the same blue pane and a corridor full of footsteps.

People moved past him with the speed and focus of commuters who knew the train was already late and still had to catch it.

A scavenger in a torn shoulder plate muttered "cycle hell" under her breath as she jogged by.

A medic in a white-striped suit walked the opposite direction with a crate marked:

EMERGENCY STABILIZATION PACKS

Max watched the crate disappear around a corner and felt the word stabilization scrape the inside of his skull.

He stood.

His knees complained.
His shoulders complained.
His nerves sent a steady wrong-note hum up the back of his neck, the kind of sensation that meant you are not recovered, even when the HUD pretended you were clocked in.

[FATIGUE STATUS]
Penalty likely on precision actions.
Suggested mitigation: impossible schedule adjustment.

"Thanks," Max muttered.

V.A.L.U. chimed in his ear at exactly the volume required to be unbearable.

"You're welcome, Asset #864! Would you like assistance building a realistic action plan?"

"No."

"Wonderful. Logging independent initiative."

He started walking toward Dock Ring C because inertia was easier than choosing.

By the time he reached the ring, shift lights had changed to cycle-open white: bright enough to reveal every stain in the metal and every twitch in a person's hands.

The overhead board screamed the new day in block text:

CYCLE OPEN
PAYMENT DUE
EXPANSION OFFERS LIVE

Below it, the disbursement crescent was already overrun.

Lanes A through D were lines.
Lane E was a shouting match.
Lane F had security drones posted in pairs.

Munch and Maxine were waiting by a cargo rail with two drink packets and no smiles.

Munch tossed one packet to Max.

"Electrolytes."

Max caught it and ripped the seal with his teeth.

The liquid tasted like mint, chalk, and regret.

"How long was I out?"

Maxine checked his wrist.

"Long enough to still be tired."

"Helpful."

"Always."

Munch pointed to Max's HUD.

"What's your open bill pane say?"

Max blinked the panel bigger.

[CYCLE BILLING STATEMENT]
Outstanding Debt: $1,000,000
Minimum Due (this cycle): $50,000
Penalty carryover: ACTIVE
Audit note count: 7
Default threshold proximity: Elevated

"Audit note count seven?" Max said.

Maxine whistled low.

"Fast learner."

"That's not learning. That's damage."

"Same metric."

Munch tapped his wrist display.

"Ignore the count. Focus the due."

Max looked toward the kiosks.

"We just paid."

"That was last cycle."

"It was six hours ago."

"In this place, six hours is an epoch."

Maxine jerked his chin toward lane C.

"Pay now before offers start sniffing."

"Offers?"

As if on cue, three new panes unfolded over Max's vision in smooth, predatory sequence.

[CYCLE OFFER]
Fast Growth Bond
Instant: +$300,000 working credit
Cost: +$600,000 debt principal
[CYCLE OFFER]
Stabilization Subscription
Suppress anomalous bleed (3 cycles)
Cost: memory artifact risk
[CYCLE OFFER]
Authorized Wake Package
Unlock 1 named recruit immediately
Cost: +$1,000,000 debt

Max's jaw clenched.

The words named recruit tried to hook something in him, the sealed pod, the palm on glass, the apology whispered into frost. For a breath he imagined paying the obscene price and forcing a door open somewhere upstream of his own helplessness.

Then the math landed: another million wasn't rescue. It was a second collar. The Company didn't sell you people; it sold you the feeling of having done something while it tightened the leash.

He blinked the offer closed harder than he meant to.

"They really don't do subtle."

Munch's voice went hard.

"Decline all three."

Maxine nodded.

"Pay due. Run clean. Live boring."

Max laughed once.

"Live boring sounds fake."

"So fake it."

He moved to lane C.

This time the line was longer and quieter.

Nobody shouted.

People held their anger in their jaws and waited for the kiosk to tell them what part of themselves to sell.

Ahead of him, an older scavenger with silver at his temples pressed his hand to the glass and whispered, "not the meds, not the meds, not the meds," like a prayer.

The kiosk answered with a bright chime.

[PAYMENT INSUFFICIENT]
Suggested: liquidate Medical Bay lease line.

The scavenger went still.

Then nodded once, like someone agreeing to his own bad idea.

He pressed accept.

The pane vanished.

He stepped away from the kiosk looking taller and emptier at the same time.

Max tried not to watch him.
He watched anyway.

The scavenger's whisper, not the meds, stayed in Max's ear like a frequency he couldn't filter. Meds meant someone somewhere needed them. The kiosk had translated need into lease line and called it help. Max wanted to call out, to offer scrap he didn't have, to do the stupid American thing and insert himself. His feet stayed in line. So did his voice. The choice felt like the cryo bay all over again: witness without leverage, shame with a receipt.

When Max reached the front, lane C recognized him immediately.

[ASSET #864]
Minimum Due: $50,000
Available immediate credits: $19,300
Deficit: $30,700

Three options expanded:

1) CREDIT + SCRAP COMBINE
2) LIQUIDATION
3) SIGN TEMPORARY PENANCE LIEN

No pure payment path.

Of course.

Max checked his scratch inventory in the corner panel:

Scrap 6,420.

Not enough.

He selected LIQUIDATION and braced for another amputation screen.

The candidates loaded:

[LIQUIDATION CANDIDATES]
- Salvage lot pending appraisal
- Remaining utility module rights (locked)
- Tool package lease (pilot grade)
- Personal inventory: corrupted data shard (high yield)

Munch appeared at his shoulder.

"Not the shard."

"I wasn't going to."

"You looked at it for too long."

Max selected tool package lease.

A warning opened:

[PILOT TOOL PACKAGE LEASE]
If liquidated:
- Action speed -8%
- Vector Nudge efficiency -1 tier modifier
- Run-time correction assist reduced

Maxine stepped in on Max's other side.

"Don't do that. Sell pending lot after appraisal."

"That's not immediate."

"Then penance lien."

Munch made a face.

"Lien means they'll tax every salvage pull this cycle."

"Better than crippling his controls."

Max stared at the three options and felt the old sensation crawl up his spine: three bad doors, and the building pretending one of them was labeled exit.

He picked TEMPORARY PENANCE LIEN.

The kiosk took a full second to respond, which in Sector 7 felt like someone holding a knife at your throat and checking a policy memo.

Then:

[PENANCE LIEN ACCEPTED]
Minimum due satisfied.

Lien terms (current cycle):
- Salvage tax: 22%
- Priority claim rights: reduced
- Auto-allocation of excess to debt service

V.A.L.U. chimed with fake warmth.

"Excellent fiscal responsibility, Asset #864."

Max stepped away before he said something that cost extra.

Maxine scanned the pane and nodded.

"Bad, not fatal."

Munch rubbed her forehead.

"It makes every run thinner."

"Everything's thinner," Max said.

Maxine looked at the cycle board.

"Then we run fatter."

Launch queue for cycle-open operations was chaos in clean lines.

Priority assets in one lane.
Debt-liens in another.
Compliance flags in a third.

Max's tag pushed him into the third.

The queue moved through a scanner gate shaped like a coffin stood upright.

Each person stepped in.
White light.
Tone.
Proceed.

When Max stepped into the gate, the white light stayed on too long.

A thin vertical pane appeared:

[COMPLIANCE PRECHECK]
Anomaly residue detected.
Cross-referencing…

Max stayed still.

The gate hummed.

Behind him, someone in line whispered, "come on, come on, come on."

The pane flickered.

[COMPLIANCE PRECHECK]
Provisional clearance granted.
Review token attached.

A new icon appeared in the top-right of Max's HUD:

a white triangle with a black dot in the center.

Review token.

Munch saw it.

"Great. They stapled a watcher to you."

Maxine grunted.

"Then give it boring footage."

They reached the launch console.

Cycle-open run selection offered five derelicts instead of one.

Most were red risk.

One amber.

One green with a tooltip that read:

LOW YIELD / LOW DRAMA

Maxine stabbed a finger at green.

"That one."

Max selected it.

V.A.L.U. sounded offended.

"Asset #864, that target underutilizes your growth trajectory."

"Good."

"Declining growth opportunities may impact long-term wealth realization."

"Put it on a mug."

"Adding to merchandise queue."

Munch blinked.

"It has a merchandise queue?"

Maxine said, "Everything has a queue."

Round 1 of the new cycle opened flat and mean.

[RUN INIT]
Target: D-04 / low-yield derelict
Energy cap: 85
Penance lien tax: 22%
Review token: active

Draw:

1) REPAIR
2) SCAN
3) EXTRACT

No shield.

Max picked SCAN first.

The beam swept the hull and painted one safe pocket and two trap pockets.

He played REPAIR.

Hull climbed.

He played EXTRACT.

Die.

[RESULT]
62/100
BREACH AVOIDED

Salvage came in thin, then got thinner under lien tax.

[SALVAGE SECURED]
Scrap +74
Metal +1
Tech +1
[LIEN TAX APPLIED]
Scrap -16
Auto-allocation: debt service buffer

Max watched the subtraction happen in real time and felt an almost nostalgic hatred for payroll systems.

"Twenty-two percent hurts," he said.

Munch replied, "That's the point."

Maxine checked the next draw.

"Keep it clean."

Round 2 draw:

1) REPAIR
2) CAUTIOUS SCAVENGE
3) EXTRACT

Max went cautious.

The run went smooth for exactly one action.

Then the review token pulsed.

[REVIEW EVENT]
Verifier ping incoming.
Maintain compliance posture.

"Compliance posture?" Max asked.

"Don't do weird physics," Munch said.

Maxine added, "And don't swear at the HUD."

Max did neither.

The verifier ping passed.

A tiny green check appeared beside the triangle icon.

He hated that he felt relieved.

Round 2 breach roll:

[RESULT]
57/100
BREACH AVOIDED

This was what Maxine meant by boring.

This was also what the station called survivable.

Round 3 draw gave him SHIELD.

He grabbed it like stolen water.

The run started to feel manageable.

Which is exactly when the whisper came back.

Not through comms.
Through HUD text.

A line flickered under the review token:

delta is not an audit team

It vanished before Max could point.

He froze.

"What?" Munch asked.

Max shook his head.

"Nothing."

Maxine stared at him for half a second, then back to the draw.

He had heard that tone before.

He didn't press.

Round 3 roll:

[RESULT]
40/100
Threshold: 35
BREACH AVOIDED

Too close.

The shield absorbed nothing because nothing hit.

It still felt used.

Round 4 and 5 passed with no breaches and no new whispers.

The review token collected green checks like a teacher's sticker chart.

Max hated it more each time.

By round 6, fatigue penalties stacked.

[FATIGUE]
Precision slip risk: elevated

His hand missed a card edge once and snapped back.

The run noticed.

[MICRO-ERROR]
Reaction window +0.4s

Everything was a metric.

Everything was billable.

Round 6 extraction roll:

[RESULT]
36/100
BREACH AVOIDED

Closer.

Maxine exhaled.

"Do not get fancy now."

"I am aggressively unfancy," Max said.

Munch pointed to the side feed.

"Lien tax is biting harder than expected."

She was right.

Every salvage line now showed two columns:

Gross.
After lien.

The second column felt like mockery.

Round 7 draw handed him EXTRACT, EXTRACT, SCAN.

The architecture of addiction again.

He took SCAN first out of spite.

The scan beam pulled up a pocket tagged:

LOW VALUE / HIGH NOISE

Noise meant anomaly risk.

He skipped it.

Played single EXTRACT.

Roll:

[RESULT]
55/100
BREACH AVOIDED

No whisper.
No menu.
No leak.

Just work.

And somehow that was worse.

Round 8 gave him no REPAIR.

He used SHIELD and EXTRACT and watched the die like it was personal.

[RESULT]
33/100
BREACH TRIGGERED

Shield took most of it.

Hull dropped hard anyway.

[HULL]
49%

Munch's voice snapped.

"Repair next or we're done."

Round 9 draw delivered REPAIR like a delayed apology.

He took it.

Hull to 56%.

Not good.
Maybe enough.

Then the review token flashed red for the first time.

[VERIFIER NOTE]
Behavioral pattern drift detected.

"What drift?" Max barked.

"Don't ask rhetorical questions at machines," Maxine said.

Max laughed despite himself.

"That was advice."

"I'm capable of growth."

Round 9 extraction:

[RESULT]
44/100
BREACH AVOIDED

Round 10 opened with a final draw that looked harmless.

1) REPAIR
2) EXTRACT
3) EXTRACT

The same shape as before.

Groundhog day with one changed variable.

He repaired.

Then took single extract.

Die.

[RESULT]
38/100
BREACH AVOIDED

He ignored the second extract card no matter how brightly it glowed.

The card pulsed.
Pulsed.
Timed out.

The run closed.

[RUN COMPLETE]
Rounds survived: 10/10
Breaches triggered: 1
Post-tax salvage retained: reduced
Review token status: pending judgment

Pending judgment.

Of course.

V.A.L.U. arrived right on schedule.

"Excellent compliance trend, Asset #864."

"Trend?"

"You are becoming statistically less surprising."

Max looked at Munch.

"I think that was an insult."

Munch said, "It was."

Maxine said, "In this place, less surprising gets you one more morning."

Back on Dock Ring C, the cycle board had changed again:

MID-CYCLE AUDITS IN PROGRESS
ENFORCEMENT TEAM ROTATION

The review token at the edge of Max's HUD turned from red to blue and opened on its own.

[REVIEW TOKEN RESOLUTION]
Outcome: CONDITIONAL PASS
Penalty carryover unchanged.
Monitoring persists.

Max exhaled.

Conditional pass.

In another life, it would have been a performance review.

In this life, it was permission to keep drowning slowly.

Munch checked his salvage totals.

"After tax and penalties, we net less than half projected."

Maxine rolled his shoulder where his armor had cracked.

"But we netted alive."

Max looked around the ring.

The same lanes.
The same kiosks.
The same drones.

Different day.
Same teeth.

Then the little red square returned.

No sound.
No warning.

Just there, bottom-right corner of the HUD.

Maxine saw Max's focus shift.

"What is it."

Max kept his eyes on the icon.

"The restricted message channel."

Munch went pale under the visor glow.

"Here?"

"No payload yet. Just icon."

Maxine stepped closer until the three of them were shoulder to shoulder by a cargo rail.

"Don't open it on the ring."

"I know."

"Say it."

"I won't open it on the ring."

Munch pointed toward the service spine.

"Safe room window in twenty if we loop the yard first."

Maxine shook his head.

"Too predictable now."

"Then where?"

Maxine's visor tilted toward a dark maintenance alcove above Salvage Bay 2.

"Comms dead zone behind recycler stack. Thirty seconds quiet if the crusher runs."

Munch grimaced.

"That is not a room."

"Neither is safety."

Max looked at the red square.

Waiting.

Like a held breath.

He looked up at the cycle board.

MID-CYCLE AUDITS IN PROGRESS.

He looked at the disbursement crescent.

He looked at the accounts recovery hatch.

Then he looked at his two companions, the only people in this sector who had ever answered his worst questions with actionable lies.

"Recycler stack," he said.

Maxine nodded once.

"Move."

They cut through bay traffic with practiced incompetence, the way locals did when they wanted to look busy, not purposeful.

Marta at Salvage Yard glanced up once as they passed and did not nod.

Good.

The recycler stack sat behind Bay 2, three stories of compactor mouths, intake belts, and warning strobes.

When the crusher cycled, it produced a roar that flattened comms for a few seconds at a time.

Maxine checked the rhythm.

Six-second roar.
Four-second gap.
Six-second roar.

"Open on roar," he said.

Max watched the red square.

Roar.

He tapped.

The pane opened in fragments.

No greeting.

No metadata this time.

Just text crawling in one line at a time:

if you want proof, look at hatch 14 during shift ghost

Roar.

Next line:

do not bring rook

Max's stomach dropped.

Next line:

he is not compromised

Roar.

Next line:

he is tracked

The pane glitched, split, and added one final line in a different font weight:

imani knows where hatch 14 is

Then it collapsed.

Red square gone.

The crusher noise faded.

Munch grabbed Max's wrist.

"What did it say?"

Max looked at Maxine.

Maxine looked back, unreadable behind the mirrored visor.

No speeches.
No exposition.

Just another choice with teeth.

Max said:

"It says shift ghost, hatch 14."

Maxine went still.

"Anything else?"

Max held his gaze for one beat too long.

"It says proof."

Munch's fingers tightened on his wrist.

"We don't do solo proof runs."

Maxine spoke quietly.

"We don't do hatch 14."

Max frowned.

"You know it?"

"Everyone knows it."

"Then tell me."

Maxine shook his head once.

"Hard-won."

The crusher roared again.

Munch leaned in, voice low.

"Shift ghost is the thirty-minute overlap when one watch clocks out and the next hasn't fully synced route maps. Half the cameras cache. Half the drones reroute."

Max looked at her.

"And hatch 14?"

Munch's eyes flicked to Maxine, then back.

"Old pre-Company access line. Officially sealed. Unofficially not."

Maxine's jaw tightened.

"If that message told you hatch 14, someone wants us split."

Max didn't answer.

Because the message had said more than that.

Because it had drawn a line between them.

Because he had already crossed it by not repeating all of it.

The crusher cycle ended.

The bay noise came back in layers.

Maxine stepped away first.

"No hatch runs tonight."

Munch nodded.

"We burn no extra risk on mystery bait."

Max said nothing.

His HUD pulsed with one neutral reminder:

[SHIFT GHOST WINDOW]
Begins in: 01:12:44

He dismissed it.

The pane came back.

He dismissed it again.

It came back again, smaller this time, anchored to the corner.

Persistent.

Like debt.

Like loops.

Like questions that cost too much to ask.

Maxine started walking toward the bunk lanes.

Munch followed.

Max followed too.

For now.

Behind them, the recycler stack roared.

Ahead of them, shift ghost kept counting down.

They made it to bunk lanes and didn't sleep.

Maxine stripped his gauntlets, checked every seam on his armor, and sat with his back to a bulkhead like a man waiting for a door to kick in.

Munch sat at a folding table with her wrist display open, running numbers on salvage, lien tax, and debt service until the columns blurred.

Max lay down on a cot bolted to the floor and watched the ceiling rivets while his HUD clock marched toward shift ghost.

01:02:19
00:58:02
00:44:11

He closed his eyes.

He saw text.

he is tracked

He opened them again.

Maxine was still there.
Munch was still there.
The room was still small and temporary and not safe.

Max sat up.

Munch looked over without lifting her head.

"No."

"I didn't say anything."

"You made the face."

"What face?"

"The face right before you do something expensive."

Maxine didn't look up.

"She's right."

Max stood anyway.

"I need air."

Maxine laughed once.

"In Sector 7?"

Max reached for his helmet.

"Figure of speech."

Munch finally looked up, eyes sharp under sleep loss.

"If you walk to hatch 14, you walk past three camera spines and two passive sweep lanes."

Max stopped with the helmet in his hands.

"You said you weren't going."

"I said we weren't going."

Maxine stood now, fast for a big man.

"No."

Munch ignored him and kept talking to Max.

"Service route H puts you above sweep lane one. Ladder drop behind conduit panel nineteen gets you past the first passive scanner. Second scanner's blind for six seconds when ventilation dampers cycle."

Maxine stared at her.

"What are you doing."

"Mitigating stupid."

"By enabling it?"

"By preventing him from dying for free."

Max looked between them and felt the loop tighten.

Choice.
Consequences.
Bill.

He put the helmet on.

"I'm going."

Maxine took one step forward.

"Then I'm coming."

Max shook his head.

"Message said don't bring you."

"Exactly."

"It said you're tracked."

"I am tracked."

No defensiveness. No explanation.

Just fact.

Munch stood, wiped both hands on her suit, and grabbed her torch.

"Then I go with him."

Maxine started to object.

Munch cut him off.

"If this is bait, two bodies are harder to bag than one. If this is real, one witness is not enough."

Maxine's jaw worked.

"You're both idiots."

"Yes," Munch said. "Guard the lane and be useful."

Maxine looked at Max.

"If either of you misses check-in by five minutes, I light the ring on fire."

"That's probably expensive," Max said.

"So are funerals."

Service route H was darker than route G and smelled like hot coolant.

Munch moved ahead at half speed, one gloved hand on rail, counting vent pulses under her breath.

"Damper cycle in three... two... one."

A deep thunk rolled through the ducting above them.

She pointed.

"Now."

Max dropped through panel nineteen and landed in a cable trench shoulder-deep in bundled conduits marked with faded labels:

OPS MIRROR
ARCHIVE
LEGACY ACCESS

He didn't pause to read more.

They moved.

Shift ghost had started.

The station felt different during overlap: not quieter, but less coherent.

Drones ran old routes for one more pass before receiving new maps.
Cameras cached and uploaded in bursts.
People in new-shift badges walked into old-shift traffic and neither side yielded.

For thirty minutes, the machine blinked.

Hatch 14 sat at the end of a dead maintenance spur behind two locked cages and one "SEALED" stencil that had been painted over so many times the word had texture.

The hatch itself was matte black, round-edged, and old enough that its bolts were real, not smart.

A reader plate sat to the left.

No company logo.
No safety icon.

Just three letters:

LCL

Munch crouched at the reader and pulled a bypass probe from her sleeve.

"Keep watch."

Max watched the corridor.

Two workers passed the spur without looking in.
A drone paused at the corner, lens sweeping, then rolled on.

Munch's tool clicked once.
Twice.

The hatch clicked back.

She looked up at Max.

"You hear that?"

Max listened.

A faint whine from inside.
Not machinery.
Not exactly.

Like audio fed through too many copies.

"Yeah."

Munch opened the hatch six inches.

Cold air spilled out.

Not station cold.
Server room cold.

She widened the gap and motioned him through.

Inside was a narrow chamber with rack towers on both walls, half of them dark, half with old status LEDs pulsing in asynchronous patterns.

At the far end sat a single terminal, live, displaying a login pane in a font Max recognized from nowhere and everywhere.

He stepped closer.

The terminal woke to proximity.

[LOCAL NODE / LEGACY CLUSTER]
User: ________
Passphrase: ________

Below it, one line of helper text:

If you remember rain, you're already authenticated.

Max went still.

Munch said, very quietly:

"Don't touch."

Max didn't touch.

The terminal touched him first.

The user field auto-filled:

MAX_864

The passphrase field filled with black dots faster than he could count.

The login pane vanished.

Munch swore.

New text flooded the screen.

Not paragraphs.
Rows.
Records.

[MIRROR INDEX]
MAX_102 ... TERMINATED
MAX_233 ... DEFAULT RECOVERY
MAX_377 ... COGNITIVE PACK APPLIED
MAX_612 ... MISSING / UNRESOLVED
MAX_864 ... ACTIVE

Max's knees nearly gave.

"Not the first Max," he said.

Munch's face had gone colorless.

"No."

He scanned the lines again and saw timestamps beside each.

Different cycles.
Different outcomes.

Same designation family.

MAX_###.

Same job.

Same debt bracket.

Another pane opened over the index.

[WARNING]
Local node access triggers audit callback in 00:00:20

Munch grabbed his sleeve.

"Take one thing and go."

Max moved to the keyboard.

Old mechanical keys.
Real travel.
No haptic bullshit.

He typed the first command that came from muscle memory instead of thought:

export latest active profile

The terminal hesitated.

Then output:

[EXPORT]
Payload size exceeds local device limit.
Suggested: text digest / visual capture / direct relay (blocked)

"Visual capture," Munch snapped.

Max opened his wrist cam and aimed it at the index screen.

Capture failed.

[CAPTURE BLOCKED]
Rights scope insufficient.

He swore.

Munch shoved past him, slapped a magnetic clip on the terminal casing, and toggled her torch.

The clip sparked.

For one second the terminal's rights block dropped.

"Now."

Max fired three quick frames.

This time:

[CAPTURE SAVED]
3 files

The callback timer hit five seconds.

The whole room changed tone.

A soft three-note chime played from somewhere in the rack wall.

[AUDIT CALLBACK]
Initiated.

Munch grabbed his arm.

"Move."

They bolted for the hatch.

Behind them, rack lights synchronized for the first time, all pulsing in the same rhythm like a heartbeat.

In the corridor, a drone turned the corner exactly as they emerged.

Its lens snapped to both of them.

[IDENTIFICATION]
Unscheduled presence.
Stand by.

Munch did not stand by.

She fired the torch at the wall conduit above the drone.

The conduit popped, spraying bright coolant vapor.

The drone's lens fogged white for half a second.

Max used the half second.

Vector Nudge.

He pushed a hand-sized momentum field under the drone's wheel cluster.

The drone skidded sideways into the rail and spun.

"Run," Munch said.

They ran.

Down spur.
Up ladder.
Across catwalk.
Through two maintenance doors that screamed when opened.

Behind them, station speakers woke up.

Not full alarm.
Not yet.

Targeted callout:

[NOTICE]
Maintenance access anomaly detected in Sector 7 service spine.
Authorized personnel continue normal operations.

"That means us," Max said between breaths.

"That means everyone but us," Munch replied.

They hit bunk lane with lungs burning and helmets fogged at the edges.

Maxine was where they left him, posted at the corner, weapon out and patience gone.

"You took eight extra minutes."

Munch bent over with hands on knees, sucking air.

"We got proof."

Max pulled up the captures.

Three images.
Blurry at the edges.
Readable in the center.

Maxine looked.

Did not react.

Looked again.

Still did not react.

Then:

"Shit."

Max almost smiled.

It was the first honest review he'd gotten all day.

Maxine zoomed image two.

MAX_377 entry ... COGNITIVE PACK APPLIED

"They route people through packs until their trace pattern behaves," Maxine said.

Munch nodded.

"Or until memory damage makes them easier to classify."

Max pointed to MAX_612 ... MISSING / UNRESOLVED.

"What does unresolved mean?"

Maxine answered too fast.

"It means don't become a footnote."

Before Max could push further, all three HUDs flashed at once.

[IMMEDIATE REVIEW SUMMONS]
Asset #864 report to Compliance Bay 3.
Cause: Maintenance anomaly correlation.
Failure to report escalates to active containment.

Maxine cursed.

"Callback hit faster than expected."

Munch killed the capture pane instantly.

"Split."

Max blinked.

"What?"

"If we all walk in together, they tie us together."

Maxine grabbed Max's shoulder.

"You go to Bay 3 and act dumb."

"I am dumb."

"Act dumber."

Munch was already moving toward the opposite corridor.

"We'll wipe traces from your route."

"How?"

She didn't look back.

"Hard-won."

Maxine leaned close enough for private comms.

"Do not mention hatch 14."

"What if they already know?"

"Then make them prove it."

He pushed Max toward the compliance lane and vanished into crowd flow like he'd never been there.

Compliance Bay 3 was brighter than Bay 1.

Same table.
Same camera dome.
Different officers.

Officer with a thin scar across his chin.
Officer with mirrored visor and no visible badge.

Scar-Chin Officer spoke first.

"Asset #864, sit."

Max sat.

The chair was colder than the room.

Scar-Chin placed a tablet on the table and rotated it.

[CORRELATION ALERT]
Maintenance anomaly
Unauthorized route deviation
Signal disruption event

"Where were you at 03:11 local?" Scar-Chin asked.

Max shrugged.

"Service corridor. Looking for a quiet place to throw up."

Scar-Chin did not smile.

"And did you access any sealed hatches?"

Max let a beat pass.

"I don't know hatch numbers."

"That was not the question."

"Then no."

Mirrored-Visor Officer finally spoke.

"Your anomaly profile suggests recurring interface contamination."

"Your station suggests recurring debt contamination."

Scar-Chin tapped once.

[CONDUCT WARNING]

"Keep answers concise," he said.

"Ask concise questions."

Another tap.

[CONDUCT WARNING]
Tier 2

Max swallowed back whatever came next.

Scar-Chin slid a new pane onto the slab:

[OPTION]
Accept Cognitive Stabilization Pack now.
Conduct warnings cleared.
Review intensity reduced.

Same offer.
Different room.

Max looked at the pane and thought of the mirror index.
MAX_377.
Pack applied.

He thought of the rooftop rain memory and how thin it already felt at the edges.

"No."

Scar-Chin's expression did not change.

"Reason?"

"I prefer my problems unsuppressed."

Mirrored-Visor tilted his head.

"Interesting choice for an asset in your bracket."

Scar-Chin made one last note.

"Then we proceed with enhanced monitoring."

Tablet flashed:

[PENALTY]
Review token upgraded: TRACKED ACTIVE
Passive route logging: enabled
Randomized verifier prompts: enabled

Max almost laughed.

"You're already tracking me."

"Now we're honest about it," Scar-Chin said.

He stood.

"Review complete."

Max stood too.

"Can I ask one thing?"

Scar-Chin paused.

"No."

Max nodded once.

"Concise."

Mirrored-Visor's head turned a fraction, as if that almost amused him.

Almost.

When Max got back to bunk lanes, Maxine and Munch were waiting in a shadow between two dead vending units.

Munch looked him over.

"You intact?"

"Mostly."

"New penalties?"

"Tracked active token."

Maxine cursed under his breath.

"That's bad."

"I gathered."

Munch asked the question without asking it.

"Did they mention hatch 14?"

"No."

Maxine exhaled.

"Then we still own the secret for one more cycle."

Max pulled up the captures again, keeping brightness low.

The three of them huddled around the tiny images while station traffic moved past them like weather around a rock.

Image one: mirror index.
Image two: MAX_377.
Image three: system footer with process tag:

DELTA_MIRROR_ROUTER

Munch tapped the tag.

"That's not an audit team name. That's process architecture."

Max said, "Message said Delta isn't a team."

Maxine looked up sharply.

"Message said what else?"

Max held his gaze.

This was the line.

He crossed it.

"It said not to bring you. Said you're tracked. Not compromised."

Maxine stared at him for two long beats.

Then nodded once.

"Fair."

Munch blinked.

"You knew?"

Maxine shrugged.

"I suspected."

"Why didn't you say?"

"Because if I said it, it became strategy. If it was only suspicion, it stayed noise."

Max rubbed his face.

"Everything in this place is noise until it can bill you."

Munch straightened.

"Then we use noise."

Maxine frowned.

"How?"

"Decoys. False routes. Burner comms."

Max stared.

"We have burner comms?"

Munch looked offended.

"I'm not a child."

Maxine gave a dry nod.

"She has burner comms."

Munch pointed between them.

"Next cycle we run split ingress on purpose. Maxine gets seen on main lane. Max and I route service spine."

Maxine objected immediately.

"No. If I stay visible every cycle, they learn pattern."

"They already know your pattern."

"Then we change mine and keep his unstable."

Max raised both hands.

"I love being discussed like a weather system."

Munch ignored him and kept arguing with Maxine in clipped tactical bursts:

"Main lane visible."
"Too clean."
"Service drop?"
"Too hot after tonight."
"Yard bypass?"
"Scanner net upgraded."

Max listened until the words stopped being words and became movement.

This was what no-exposition looked like in real life: two people arguing procedures because procedures were the only safe way to say fear out loud.

Finally Maxine looked at Max.

"Decision."

Max looked at the captures.
At the process tag.
At the names.

Not first Max.

"We keep learning," he said. "Without taking stabilization. Without selling the shard."

Munch nodded.

"Agreed."

Maxine nodded slower.

"Agreed."

Then Max added:

"And we run hatch 14 again."

Silence.

Munch recovered first.

"Not next cycle."

"Soon."

Maxine looked like he wanted to bite through steel.

"Soon gets people disappeared."

"Soon gets answers before they patch the hole."

Munch stared at Max, then at the captures, then back.

"One more clean cycle," she said. "Then we decide."

Maxine gave a single hard nod.

"One cycle."

Max accepted the deal because it was the only one on the table that didn't end tonight with someone dragged through Accounts Recovery.

The cycle board pinged midnight handoff.

Not a real midnight.
Cycle midnight.

Overhead text shifted:

SHIFT GHOST CLOSED
ROUTE MAPS SYNCED

The station clicked into tighter order.

Drones resumed predictable lanes.
Cameras returned to full ping.
People slowed from frantic to merely exhausted.

Max's HUD opened one final pane before sleep claimed anything.

[TRACKED ACTIVE TOKEN]
Status: live
Advisory: irregularity invites scrutiny

Below it, almost hidden:

[CAPTURE CACHE]
3 restricted files pending integrity loss in 05:00:00

Munch saw the timer.

"Five-hour decay."

Maxine swore.

"Then we archive now."

"Where?"

Munch answered:

"Off-net physical."
